name: oti-historical-analogues
description: Curated 30+ event corpus of macro-history analogues, retrieved via two-stage hybrid (Jaccard tags + voyage-4-large + History Rhymes macro fusion + Voyage rerank). Memory, not prediction.
long_description:
OTI is a historical-analogue research engine for macro markets. Type a market event in plain English; the server returns the 3 best-fitting historical regimes from a curated 39-event corpus (1929–2025), with a 4th 'near-miss-resolved-oppositely' contrastive analogue, structural-look-ahead-defended synthesis, and conformal-calibrated 80% intervals on each asset's @1m return. The corpus is open-source and community-contributable.

This MCP server exposes three tools: `search_analogues` (top-K events for a free-text query), `get_event` (full payload by id, with point-in-time view option), and `list_events` (corpus discovery). search_analogues runs the full retrieval pipeline (tag → embed → fused cosine → rerank) but skips synthesis to stay sub-second inside an agent loop.

screenshots.caption: OTI Daily — today's regime fingerprint with the 3 closest historical analogues + 1 contrastive near-miss
User-event brief with calibrated @1m intervals and 'show your work' retrieval audit

tools.name: search_analogues
tools.description: Find historical macro events analogous to a user-described event. Returns top-K events with point-in-time narrative, t=0 market reaction, and retrieval scores. Schema-constrained to OTI's curated corpus.

tools.name: get_event
tools.description: Return a single corpus event by id. Use includeHindsight=true for the full payload; default includeHindsight=false returns the point-in-time view safe for forward-looking reasoning.

tools.name: list_events
tools.description: List every event in the corpus with id, title, date, region, triggerType, regimeTags. Discovery surface before search_analogues.

data_provenance: All asset moves are programmatically refreshed from FRED + Stooq (run `pnpm refresh-prices`); failed-trade quotes are flagged with provenance ∈ {verified, paraphrase_with_source, paraphrase_no_source}; corpus version hash returned on every tool response.

privacy.logs_anonymous_telemetry: Langfuse OTel spans, user-query content not retained
